source: Meta-Graph Based Recommendation Fusion over Heterogeneous Information Networks

concepts.finding: Meta-graph (multiple meta-paths combined) for heterogeneous network embedding. Weights different meta-paths via a learned fusion mechanism. Outperforms single meta-path methods. Meta-graph captures compound relationships that single meta-paths miss.
concepts.implication: Combining multiple meta-paths (Keyword-Document-Intent, Keyword-Session-Keyword, Keyword-Category-Intent) produces richer keyword embeddings than any single meta-path. Learned fusion weights reveal which relationships are most informative.
concepts.open_question: Which meta-paths contribute most to keyword-intent understanding? Can attention-based fusion identify the best combination automatically?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A meta-graph fusion model combining co-occurrence, session, document, and category meta-paths outperforms any single meta-path for keyword-intent clustering.
